## Key Takeaways

- Chase Ultimate Rewards points can be redeemed for travel through Chase Travel or transferred to partners like Marriott, Southwest, and United.
- Transferring to airline partner programs like KrisFlyer Miles or MileagePlus Miles often provides the best value, especially for premium cabins or international flights.
- Points can also be used for gift cards or statement credits, though transferring for travel redemptions typically yields the highest return on value.

Chase Ultimate Rewards points are easy to earn and offer great redemption opportunities. To get the best value and use your points for maximum value, you’ll certainly want to transfer them to one of Chase’s airline and hotel partners for a travel redemption.

**Don’t be tempted to use your Chase Ultimate Rewards points for cash-back or on merchandise** through its own shopping portal, since your return won’t be as high. You have the option of using your points in the [Chase Travel portal](https://upgradedpoints.com/travel/chase-ultimate-rewards-travel-portal/), but partner transfers will generally be much more valuable.

In this post, you’ll find the best ways to use Chase Ultimate Rewards points for travel redemptions with many of the Chase Ultimate Rewards program partners. These travel partners will maximize the value you can obtain when redeeming for flights and overnight stays at hotels.

Great award redemptions emerge and disappear all the time. For now, these are the best-known redemptions out there for Chase Ultimate Rewards points.

## Transfer Chase Ultimate Rewards Points to Airline and Hotel Partners

You can redeem Ultimate Rewards points for travel booked through [Chase Travel](https://upgradedpoints.com/travel/chase-ultimate-rewards-travel-portal/) with **up to 2 cents per point in value** on select flights and hotels through Points Boost, or opt for [Pay Yourself Back](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/chase-pay-yourself-back-is-it-worth-it/), statement credits, gift cards, [charitable donations](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/how-to-donate-miles-points-credit-card-rewards-to-charity/) (through December 31, 2026), gift cards, and merchandise.

However, the most lucrative redemptions are available when **[transferring your Chase Ultimate Rewards points](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/chase-ultimate-rewards-transfer-partners/) to a partner loyalty program**, which is why these points are highly valuable. Ultimate Rewards transfer to hotel and airline programs at a 1:1 ratio (see below).

## The 35 Best Ways To Redeem Your Chase Ultimate Rewards Points for Flights

The best way to get maximum value from Chase Ultimate Rewards is to **redeem them for flights**. Transfer Ultimate Rewards to Chase airline partners for exceptional flight redemptions in first class, business, and more.

These are some of the best redemptions you can make with Chase Ultimate Rewards airline partners.

### 1\. Fly Singapore Suites in an Airbus A380 (KrisFlyer Miles)

[![Singapore Airlines new A380 First Suite Double Bed](https://upgradedpoints.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/11/33-Singapore-Airlines-new-A380-First-Suite-Double-Bed.jpg?auto=webp&disable=upscale&width=1200)](https://upgradedpoints.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/11/33-Singapore-Airlines-new-A380-First-Suite-Double-Bed.jpg?auto=webp&disable=upscale) Singapore Airlines’ new A380 Suites features a double bed and separate lounge chair. Image Credit: [Greg Stone](https://upgradedpoints.com/author/greg/)

Most people transfer points to the [Singapore KrisFlyer miles program](https://upgradedpoints.com/travel/airlines/how-to-redeem-singapore-airlines-krisflyer-miles/) because they want to experience Singapore Suites, Singapore Airlines’ first class luxury double bed in the sky. You won’t experience a product like this on any other airline.

The least expensive way to experience Singapore Suites is on its Singapore (SIN) to Hong Kong (HKG) route. **A one-way Suites Class ticket will only cost 47,500 KrisFlyer miles**. Note that Singapore Suites Class is only available on the Airbus A380 aircraft.

When you save up enough Chase Ultimate Rewards points and find availability for Singapore Suites class, definitely book a ticket … or 2!

Another highlight of [Singapore Airlines’ KrisFlyer program](https://upgradedpoints.com/travel/airlines/singapore-airlines-krisflyer-loyalty-program/) is that it’s a **transfer partner of all of the major transferable points currencies**, including [American Express Membership Rewards](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/american-express-membership-rewards-review/), [Capital One Miles](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/capital-one-miles-program-review/), [Chase Ultimate Rewards](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/chase-ultimate-rewards-review/), [Citi ThankYou Rewards](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/citi-thankyou-rewards-review/), and Marriott Bonvoy.

If you are short on Chase Ultimate Rewards points, there are other opportunities to [earn the additional KrisFlyer miles](https://upgradedpoints.com/travel/airlines/how-to-earn-singapore-airlines-krisflyer-miles/).

**Hot Tip:**

It’s tough to find 2 or more seats on the same flight for Singapore Suites class. You have 2 options if you’re looking to book travel on more than 1 ticket: book a second ticket for more miles or waitlist for a second seat at the same price.

**Need help booking?** See our step-by-step guide for [booking Singapore Suites or first class](https://upgradedpoints.com/travel/airlines/best-ways-to-book-singapore-airlines-first-class/).

### 2\. Round-Trip First Class to Tokyo From the U.S. on All Nippon Airways (Virgin Points)

## The 6 Best Ways To Redeem Your Chase Ultimate Rewards Points for Hotel Stays

Chase hotel partners can offer excellent rewards redemptions, from luxury overwater stays to room upgrades. See some of our favorite ways to use Chase Ultimate Rewards points with hotel partners.

### 1\. Book Luxury Hyatt Hotels (World of Hyatt)

![Park Hyatt Zurich Exterior](https://upgradedpoints.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/06/Park-Hyatt-Zurich-Exterior.jpg?auto=webp&disable=upscale&width=1200)You’ll love your free stay at the Park Hyatt Zürich! Image Credit: Katie Seemann

The award chart for World of Hyatt is **one of the best for hotel stays**. You can use your World of Hyatt points to stay at a standard room in a Category 1 Hyatt hotel for as few as 3,500 points.

These low-category hotels are not usually expensive, so even if it is good value to use your points, it might be worth it to save them for a luxury redemption.

For example, you can [redeem your Hyatt points for luxury hotels](https://upgradedpoints.com/travel/hotels/best-hyatt-luxury-hotels-to-book-with-points/), like the [Park Hyatt Maldives](https://www.hyatt.com/en-US/hotel/maldives/park-hyatt-maldives-hadahaa/mldph) or the Park Hyatt St. Kitts Christophe Harbour for 25,000 to 35,000 points per night. These hotels **can easily be over $1,000 per night otherwise**. Or, you could stay at the [Andaz 5th Avenue](https://www.hyatt.com/andaz/nycam-andaz-5th-avenue) in New York City for 25,000 to 35,000 points per night.

### 2\. Book All-Inclusive Hyatt Hotels (World of Hyatt)

![Hyatt Ziva Los Cabos](https://upgradedpoints.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/11/Hyatt-Ziva-Los-Cabos.jpg?auto=webp&disable=upscale&width=1200)If you use your points at the Hyatt Ziva Los Cabos, you’ll have food and drinks included in your stay! Image Credit: Katie Seemann

Hyatt also has a handful of all-inclusive brands (such as [Hyatt Ziva](https://upgradedpoints.com/travel/hotels/hyatt-ziva-guide/) and [Hyatt Zilara](https://upgradedpoints.com/travel/hotels/hyatt-zilara-guide/)) where you can use your points. Depending on the hotel and travel dates, a standard room with double occupancy will cost anywhere from 12,000 to 50,000 points per night during standard dates. A night in a suite at an all-inclusive property will cost 24,000 to 80,000 points during standard dates for double occupancy.

You can add extra people (up to 4 total) for 7,500 to 25,000 points per night during standard dates. You’ll have **all of your meals and drinks included with your room at these hotels**, so while the cost is quite high, consider the whole package value.

We’re big fans of the Hotel Ziva Cancun and Hotel Zilara Cap Cana.

In addition, you can redeem World of Hyatt points for stays at **AMResorts** properties, including Secrets, Zoetry, Dreams, and more! Note that all of Hyatt’s all-inclusive properties follow their own [award chart](https://world.hyatt.com/content/gp/en/rewards/free-nights-upgrades.html#inclusive).

### 3\. Upgrade Your Room at a Hyatt (World of Hyatt)

![Hyatt Regency Chicago club lounge sitting area](https://upgradedpoints.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/Hyatt-Regency-Chicago-club-lounge-sitting-area.jpg?auto=webp&disable=upscale&width=1200)Hyatt Regency Chicago Club Lounge. Image Credit: Katie Seemann

Hyatt’s upgrade chart is reasonable and can present some great value. An **upgrade to a Club Room costs 3,000 points** per night. This will give you access to the lounge, which will probably mean some free snacks and drinks. What an easy way to save yourself some money at the bar.

It’s also possible to use your World of Hyatt points to upgrade your room to a **suite.** The cost is only **6,000 points** per night, and you’ll also have access to the hotel lounge if your hotel has one.

### 4\. Save Some Points With a Points + Cash Rate (World of Hyatt)

In addition to having a great all-points award chart, Hyatt also gives you the option to redeem [points and cash](https://world.hyatt.com/content/gp/en/rewards/free-nights-upgrades.html) for a stay. The points cost is half of what the cost would be if only using points, and there is a cash portion to make up the difference.

The cash price you pay is **50% to 70% of the standard rate**.

Virgin Atlantic doesn’t always have the best award redemption charts for flights. With the exception of using Virgin Atlantic for ANA redemptions, [Virgin Atlantic redemptions](https://upgradedpoints.com/travel/airlines/how-to-redeem-virgin-atlantic-miles/) are often expensive and not worth it.

However, one of the better redemptions you can make with Virgin Points has nothing to do with flying. Virgin Atlantic has partnered with [Hilton Honors](https://www.virgin-atlantic.com/us/en/flying-club/flying-club-partners/hotels/hilton-worldwide.html) and offers a 2:3 transfer ratio between the programs.

With Hilton, 15,000 points are enough for a 3-night stay at hotels charging 5,000 points per night. These hotels are lightly sprinkled around the world, mainly South America.

We like the [Motto by Hilton New York City Times Square](https://upgradedpoints.com/travel/hotels/best-motto-by-hilton-hotels/) and [Conrad Hong Kong](https://upgradedpoints.com/travel/hotels/best-ways-to-redeem-hilton-points/), among others.

## How To Transfer Your Ultimate Rewards to Airline and Hotel Transfer Partners

**1\.** Navigate to [Chase](https://chase.com/)’s website and log in to your account.

[![Chase Bank Sign-in](https://upgradedpoints.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/09/Screen-Shot-2016-09-17-at-5.03.39-PM.png?auto=webp&disable=upscale&width=1200)](https://upgradedpoints.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/09/Screen-Shot-2016-09-17-at-5.03.39-PM.png?auto=webp&disable=upscale) Image Credit: Chase

**2.** Select **Go to Ultimate Rewards** on the bottom-left side of your account screen.

[![Chase Ultimate Rewards Balance](https://upgradedpoints.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/01/Chase-Ultimate-Rewards-Balance.jpg?auto=webp&disable=upscale&width=1200)](https://upgradedpoints.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/01/Chase-Ultimate-Rewards-Balance.jpg?auto=webp&disable=upscale) Image Credit: Chase

**3.** Select the account with the points you would like to use.

[![Chase Ultimate Rewards cards and balances](https://upgradedpoints.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/09/Screen-Shot-2016-09-17-at-5.png?auto=webp&disable=upscale&width=1200)](https://upgradedpoints.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/09/Screen-Shot-2016-09-17-at-5.png?auto=webp&disable=upscale) Image Credit: Chase

**4.** At the top of the screen, hover over the **Use Points** drop-down menu and select **Transfer to Travel Partners**.

[![Chase Ultimate Rewards Portal](https://upgradedpoints.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/01/Chase-Ultimate-Rewards-Portal.jpg?auto=webp&disable=upscale&width=1200)](https://upgradedpoints.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/01/Chase-Ultimate-Rewards-Portal.jpg?auto=webp&disable=upscale) Image Credit: Chase

**5\.** Select the program you would like to transfer points to. In this case, we will select [Air France-KLM Flying Blue](https://upgradedpoints.com/travel/airlines/flying-blue-air-france-klm-loyalty-program/).

**6\.** Add your member ID and select **Continue.**

[![Air France KLM Flying Blue Chase Transfer](https://upgradedpoints.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/01/Air-France-KLM-Flying-Blue-Chase-Transfer.jpg?auto=webp&disable=upscale&width=1200)](https://upgradedpoints.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/01/Air-France-KLM-Flying-Blue-Chase-Transfer.jpg?auto=webp&disable=upscale) Image Credit: Chase

**7.** Enter the number of miles you would like to transfer and select **Continue.**

[![Chase transfer portal Air France Flying Blue](https://upgradedpoints.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/09/Screen-Shot-2016-09-17-at-51.png?auto=webp&disable=upscale&width=1200)](https://upgradedpoints.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/09/Screen-Shot-2016-09-17-at-51.png?auto=webp&disable=upscale) Image Credit: Chase

**8.** Review your transfer details. Remember that once you initiate the transfer, you can’t return miles to your Chase account. Click **Confirm and Submit** when you’re ready to complete your transfer.

## **Final Thoughts**

Hopefully, you now know plenty more about the best ways to use Chase Ultimate Rewards points!

While you could use one of the [many online travel portals to find cheap cash flights](https://upgradedpoints.com/travel/best-websites-for-booking-cheap-flights/), use points for statement credit, or redeem for gift cards, **transferring points to partners using a Chase card offers the amazing travel opportunities** outlined above.

Chase Ultimate Rewards points will get you domestic and international flight travel and hotel stays across the world for maximum value when transferring to partners — but only if applied strategically.

With a little planning, you’ll be flying to Australia and Fiji for dirt cheap, or enjoying a trip across the U.S. with your family for far fewer points than you would expect.

If you need more of these points for your next award, you can utilize an Ultimate Rewards card, such as the [Chase Sapphire Preferred card](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/reviews/chase-sapphire-preferred-card/), [Freedom Unlimited card](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/reviews/chase-freedom-unlimited-credit-card/), or [Ink Business Preferred® Credit Card](https://upgradedpoints.com/credit-cards/reviews/chase-ink-business-preferred-credit-card/).
